# Introduction
# Prerequisites \- 

* [Harness Developer Hub IDP Documentation](https://developer.harness.io/docs/internal-developer-portal/)  
* Access to [https://workshop.harness.io](https://workshop.harness.io) via a browser 

# Accessing the Workshop Account

1. Go to [https://workshop.harness.io](https://workshop.harness.io) and log in using the credentials provided by the instructor.  
2. \<\< per email delivery or to the SE Owner \>\>  
   

***Harness Unified View \-\> Code Repository \-\>*** **\<\<PROJECT NAME\>\>**

# 

# Input Selection

* Choose a username and use that across the entire lab where you see \<***\<your-username\>\>**.* First initial and last name is a good choice for this. *Alpha characters only, no special characters or spaces*  
* For \<***\<team-name\>\>***, use the team name that your instructor provides (or use **TeamA** if no other name is provided)

# Lab 1 \- Add a Service Component \- (Import vs Manual Form)

### **Summary**: Configure a service component and associate it with a team, and create a dependency

**Learning Objective(s):**

* **Defining a service component**  
* **Defining relationships and attributes in a service component**  
* **Browsing different aspects of a component in the UI and YAML**  
  ![][image1]

**Part 1: Register dependent component \- (Git Import of pre-built file)**

1. Select Register from left Column  
2. Choose **Import from Git**  
3. Under Select Scope \-\> Choose *Project* and sub-select your project name from the list. \-\> *NEXT*  
   *![][image2]*  
4. Under Git Source \-\> Harness Code Repository  
   ![][image3]  
     
   

| Field Location | New Value |
| :---- | :---- |
| Select Git Provider | ***Harness Code Repository*** |
| Repository | idp-workshop-\<\<ProjectID\>\> |
| Git Branch | ***main*** |
| YAML Path | components/catalog-info.yaml |

   

   

5. Select **IMPORT** 

**Part 2: Create a Second Component/Service \- Use the Form for a Creation of a Service Component**

1. On the left hand side menu select the **Register** tab:  
2. Act as an Application Developer and select **Component**  
3. 

| ![][image4] |
| :---- |

5. At the Basic Details Form:

   

| Field Location | New Value |
| :---- | :---- |
| Name | ***\<\<ProjectID\>\>***\-service-dependency |
| Type | service |
| Owner | ***Kroger*** |
| Description (optional) | My first service in the IDP |
| Tags (Optional) | ***kroger*** |
| Lifecycle | experimental |

   

6. Define Scope as Project Level  
7. Select your Project Name from dropdown: **\<\<ProjectID\>\>**  
1. Link to Source Code Repository  
   1. Select **Harness Code Repository** from dropdown list  
   2. Repository:   Select **IDP-Workshop-\<\<ProjectID\>\>** repo under the project tab.  
2. Store the Component **Inline**  
3. Select the *Review YAML* button at the bottom of the page  
4. Create an additional entry in YAML for a dependency under the Spec: block  
   1. Use the name of the Service you created in Step 1\.  
   2. Example listed here:

|  *`dependsOn:    -component: jblab420ip1-service`*  |
| :---- |

      

5. Finalize with **Create Component button**  
6. View your newly created component- **View Component**

**Part 3: Review components layout**  
With your component from the previous exercise, review various aspects of the component  
**![][image5]**

***Example component view***

1. Open your dependent service catalog item  
2. In the Relations widget: Note that the component is owned by the team (group) you specified in the YAML and is dependent on your first service component  
3. Click on your first component (this will move you into the component)

4. Take note of links, source, etc that were defined in your component showing up in the UI  
5. Click on the CI/CD, Scorecard, and API tabs

# Lab 2 \- Scorecards

### **Summary**: Configure score card and weights

**Learning Objective(s):**

* **Configure a scorecard**

**Part 1: Create a scorecard**

1. On the left hand menu in Harness, select **Configure** and then **Scorecards**  
2. Click **Create New Scorecard** and configure as follows  
   

| Input | Value |
| :---- | :---- |
| Name | ***\<\<ProjectID\>\>*** |
| Description | ***\<optional\>*** |
| Kind | ***Components*** |
| Type | service |
| Owners | ***\<\<Team Owner\>\>*** |
| Tags | ***kroger*** |
| Lifecycle | experimental |
| Scope | ***Select your org/project*** |

 


3. Under **Add Checks to Scorecard** on the right, click \+ **Add Checks**  
4. In the dialog that opens, check **Select All** at the top of the screen, the click on **Add Selected Checks** at the bottom of the screen  
5. Scroll to the bottom of the page and click on **Publish Scorecard**

   **![][image6]**

**Part 2: Review scorecard**

1. Navigate to one of your services  
2. Your scorecard should appear with a grade under 10  (NOT GOOD\!)  
3. If more than one scorecard is present, chances are that someone in this workshop misconfigured their scorecard filters  
4. If the sample scorecard is still present, chances are that you misconfigured your scorecard filters  
5. Click into the scorecard tab to see what checks passed and failed, detailing out your score 

**Part 3: Change the criteria**  
Let’s fix that \<10 the easy way by changing the grading standards

1. On the left hand menu in Harness, select **Configure** and then **Scorecards**  
2. Find your scorecard and click on **Edit**  
3. Toggle **Adjust Weights** on the right hand side  
4. Set the value to **100** for the following checks  
   1. Spec owner exists  
   2. Tech Docs exists  
5. Click **Update Scorecard**  
6. Review your service’s new score. Congratulations on your passing grade\!

# 

# Lab 3 \- Self service workflows 

### **Summary**: Create a self service workflow to provision a new repository according to standardized app patterns

**Learning Objective(s):**

* **Understand the components involved in a self-service workflow**   
* **Use Harness IDP as a developer who needs to provision a new repository**

**Part 1: Review the contents of the onboard-new-app.yaml**

1. Navigate to your repository and open the contents of the **onboard-new-app.yaml**  
2. Take time to understand the template yaml and the [orchestration](https://workshop.harness.io/ng/account/IFG41DWvSnaRLOVNB2uesg/module/idp-admin/orgs/idp/projects/SelfService_Onboarding/pipelines/OnboardAppPipeline/pipeline-studio?storeType=INLINE) behind the template  
3. Let’s review as a class the contents behind both the IDP template and Harness as the orchestration mechanism

**\*Part 2: Scaffold a new repository according to your standardized app pattern via IDP (CURRENTLY GITHUB)**

1. From the Harness Internal Developer Portal, go to **Self-Service** from the left hand side menu  
2. In the **Provision a New Application Pattern** tile, click on **Execute**  
3. Enter the name of the repository you would like to create, for example ***\<team-name\>***\-***\<your-projectID\> \* all lowercase***  
4. Enter a description of the application  
5. For the Owner field, select ***Kroger***  
6. Click on **Review**, then click **Create**  
7. You will be directed to the task activity. Click **View Details** to view the pipeline execution  
8. Once the pipeline is completed, go to the [harnessworkshop](https://github.com/orgs/harnessworkshop/repositories) repo in GitHub and validate the repository was generated  
9. View the contents of your auto generated repository\! It’s magic.  
10. Go to the IDP Catalog and find your component (with the same name as your repo), which was automatically created and registered for you\!\*

# Lab 4 \- Add CI/CD

### **Summary**: Configure pipeline relationship

**Learning Objective(s):**

* **Link CI/CD results to your registered component**

**Part 1: Navigate to the CI  and CD tabs of your Services in Step 1 and Step 2 of LAB1**  
Look at the Overview tab of each and notice the CI and CD tabs to the right of the overview.   
**CI**  
**![][image7]**  
**CD**  
![][image8]  
The imported service had some pipelines connected at its origin but the second service with the dependency didn’t have a pipeline in its annotations.   Let's change that. 

**Part 2: Add CI/CD and project details to a component**

1. Open your service imported in step1 and view yaml source.   
     
2. Under **annotations**, find **harness.io/pipelines.** Copy the lines of the annotations \-\> [harness.io/pipelines](http://harness.io/pipelines):  and paste them to the service you created in step 2\.  Remember to put that service view of YAML into ‘EDIT’ mode.

For Example:

| metadata:  description: My Example service which has something to do with APIs and database.  annotations:    harness.io/pipelines: |      Deploy: https://workshop.harness.io/ng/account/IFG41DWvSnaRLOVNB2uesg/home/orgs/idp/projects/SelfService\_Onboarding/pipelines/OnboardAppPipeline/pipeline-studio?storeType=INLINE |
| :---- |

3. Save/commit/push your changes  
4. Refresh your browser tab and then click on the **CI/CD** tab in the catalog service  
5. You will see a sample list of deployments for the associated service

# CD

![][image9]

# (BONUS\*) Lab 5 \- Create a self-service workflow from scratch

### **Summary**: Create your own self-service workflow that creates a repository from scratch using Backstage templates, Cookie Cutter, and Harness as the orchestration tool

**Learning Objective(s):**

* **Create a Backstage template.yaml**  
* **Import the template into the Software Catalog (as done in Lab 1\)**  
* **Create a Harness pipeline which contains the logic of what actions will be taken in the self-service workflow**

**Useful links:**

* [How to write IDP templates](https://developer.harness.io/docs/internal-developer-portal/flows/service-onboarding-pipelines#how-to-write-idp-templates)  
* [Supported custom actions](https://developer.harness.io/docs/internal-developer-portal/flows/custom-actions/#custom-actions-usage-limitations)


**Part 1: Create an onboarding pipeline**

1. From the Internal Developer Portal, click on **Configure** on the left hand side menu  
2. Click on the scope selector and select the project that was created for your as part of the workshop  
   ![][image10]  
3. From the left hand menu, select **Pipelines**   
4. Click **\+ Create a Pipeline**, enter the following values, then click **Start**  
   

| Input | Value | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Name | Onboard New Data Science Service |  |
| How do you want to setup your pipeline | Inline | This indicates that Harness (rather than Git) will be the source of truth for the pipeline |

   

5. Navigate to the Variables in the far right tooltip bar  
6. Add two variables and set the values to “Runtime Inputs”  
   * **project\_name**  
   * **feature\_branch**

**![][image11]**

7. From Pipeline Studio, click **Add Stage** and select **Developer Portal** as the Stage Type  
8. Enter the following values and click on **Set Up Stage**

| Input | Value | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Stage Name | Service Onboarding |  |

9. Configure the **Developer Portal** Stage with the following

   **Infrastructure**

| Input | Value | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Infrastructure | Cloud |  |
| Operating System | Linux |  |
| Architecture | AMD64 |  |

   

   

   **Execution**

   * Select **Add Step**, then **Add Step** again, then select **Cookiecutter** from the Step Library and configure with the following

| Input | Value | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Name | Cookie Cutter |  |
| Repository Type | Public |  |
| Provide URL of cookiecutter template | https://github.com/harnessworkshop/idp-workshop-cookiecutter | This is a public repository we will use as our cookie cutter template to provision a new repo for service onboarding |
| **\+New key-value pair** | key: app\_name value: \<+pipeline.variables.project\_name\> |  |

* Select **Add Step**, then **Add Step** again, then select **Create Repo** from the Step Library and configure with the following

| Input | Value | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Name | Create Repo |  |
| Select Git Provider | Third-party Git provider GitHub | Choose which SCM provider to create the new repository in |
| Repository Type | Public |  |
| Select codebase repo connector | idp |  |
| Organization | harnessworkshop | Organization the repository will be created in |
| Repository Name | \<+pipeline.variables.project\_name\> | Use Harness variables to populate the project name |
| Repository Description (optional) | Generated by Harness IDP |  |
| Default branch | develop |  |

* Select **Add Step**, then **Add Step** again, then select **Create Catalog** from the Step Library and configure with the following

| Input | Value | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Name | Create Service YAML |  |
| File Name | bonus-service-***\<your-username\>***.yaml |  |
| Path | \<+pipeline.variables.project\_name\> |  |
| File Content  | apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1 kind: Component metadata:   name: \<+pipeline.variables.project\_name\>   description: Pipeline component created   annotations:     backstage.io/techdocs-ref: dir:../   tags:     \- ***\<your-username\>*** spec:   type: service   owner: ***\<team-name\>***   lifecycle: experimental  |  You can use the YAML view to validate any paste errors |

* Select **Add Step**, then **Add Step** again, then select **DirectPush** from the Step Library and configure with the following

| Input | Value | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Name | Push Repo |  |
| Select Git Provider | Third-party Git provider GitHub | Choose which SCM provider to create the new repository in |
| Codebase repo connector | idp |  |
| Organization | harnessworkshop | Organization the repository will be created in |
| Repository Name | \<+pipeline.variables.project\_name\> | Use Harness variables to populate the project name |
| Code Directory | \<+pipeline.variables.project\_name\> |  |
| Branch name | \<+pipeline.variables.feature\_branch\> |  |
| Allow Force Push | ✅ | Will allow an overwrite for our first push |


  * Select **Add Step**, then **Add Step** again, then select **Register Catalog** from the Step Library and configure with the following

| Input | Value | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Name | Register Catalog |  |
| Select Git Provider | Third-party Git provider GitHub | Choose which SCM provider to create the new repository in |
| Select Connector | idp |  |
| Organization | harnessworkshop | Organization the repository will be created in |
| Repository Name | \<+pipeline.variables.project\_name\> | Use Harness variables to populate the project name |
| Branch name | \<+pipeline.variables.feature\_branch\> |  |
| File Path | bonus-service-***\<your-username\>***.yaml |  |

10. Click **Save** to save the pipeline  
11. Copy the URL from your browser tab. This will be used in Part 2\.

**Part 2: Update the onboarding template YAML**

1. Open **bonus-onboarding-template.yaml** for editing  
2. Find all instances of **yournamehere** and replace with ***\<your-username\>***  
   * This should be in *metadata.name* and *metadata.tags*  
3. Update line **51** and replace **\<input onboarding pipeline URL\>** with the URL you copied at the end of Part 1\.  
4. Save/commit/push your changes  
5. Copy the GitHub URL for the *bonus-onboarding-template.yaml* you just updated 

**Part 3: Register the Workflow**

1. On the left hand sub-menu, select **Register**  
2. In the URL field, add the *bonus-onboarding-template.yaml* location you copied in the last step of Part 2 above.  
3. Click **Analyze**, then click **Import**  
   

**Part 4: Execute Self Service Workflow**

* On the left hand sub-menu, select **Self-Service**  
* Find the Workflow you just created  
  * Look for **Create a cookicutter datascience app** with your username as a tag  
* Click on **Select** for that workflow  
* Provide a name for the new repo to create and a name for the feature branch  
* Click on **Next**  
* Choose ***\<team-name\>*** as the owner  
* Click on **Review**, then **Create**  
* You will be directed to the task activity. Click **Show Logs** and the click on the link in the logs to view the pipeline execution  
* Once the pipeline is completed, go to the [harnessworkshop](https://github.com/orgs/harnessworkshop/repositories) organization in GitHub and validate the repository was generated  
* View the contents of your auto generated repository\! It’s magic.
